stdout:
## Acceptance Criteria

Pass.

- `crates/jit/src/config.rs:443` adds `ItemKindSource`, allowing `source` to be either a markdown path or structured TOML descriptor.
- `crates/jit/src/config.rs:588` defines the descriptor fields: `toml`, `table`, `id-field`, `text-field`, and `link-fields`.
- `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1311` implements the generic pure loader from TOML content into `RawScopeItem` rows.
- `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:224` reads descriptor TOML through `self.storage.read_repo_file`, not direct filesystem access.
- `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:756` verifies a non-invariant `policy` registry-first kind indexes from a custom TOML source, including self-id, text, and link namespace mapping.
- Existing invariant indexing remains routed through the typed registry at `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:211`.

## Architecture And Separation Of Concerns

Pass. The domain loader in `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1311` receives TOML content as a string and performs no I/O. The command layer orchestrates config, storage, and domain projection in `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:197`. TOML file reads go through the `IssueStore` boundary at `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:224`.

## Functional Paradigm

Pass. The generic loader uses iterator-based projection and collection in `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1334` and `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1350`. The command layer uses imperative orchestration in `project_items`, which is appropriate at the storage boundary.

## Correctness And Invariants

Pass. No file-write, event-log, gate-transition, assignee, label, PID, or unsafe-code invariant is changed by this patch. The new registry-first path remains project-scoped and feeds all rows through `index_project_sources` at `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:285`, preserving shared project-scope de-duplication. Path safety for descriptor TOML is delegated to `IssueStore::read_repo_file`.

## Safety

Pass. No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed change surface.

## Testing

Pass. The provided issue context records `cargo-ci` as passed on 2026-06-26T22:23:16Z, so I did not rerun cargo tests. New relevant coverage includes:

- `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:756` for custom TOML registry indexing through storage.
- `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:791` for absent descriptor TOML handling.
- `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1982` through `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:2052` for TOML field/link/error edge cases.

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed locally with enforcement-drift warnings only, which are allowed by the review instructions.

## Error Handling

Pass. New TOML failures are typed `thiserror` variants in `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:404`, `:422`, and `:437`, with kind/table/field context. The follow-up commit removed the prior library `expect()` in `project_items`; the descriptor is now bound with `else if let Some(descriptor)` at `crates/jit/src/commands/item.rs:216`.

## CLI Contract

Pass. No new CLI command was added. Existing item commands remain behind the existing output paths and machine-readable support.

## Documentation

Pass. New public surfaces include doc comments and examples: `ItemKindSource` at `crates/jit/src/config.rs:443`, `TomlSourceDescriptor` at `crates/jit/src/config.rs:556`, `ItemKind::toml_source` at `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:811`, and `load_toml_scope_items` at `crates/jit/src/domain/item.rs:1264`.

## Prior Review Feedback

No prior `run_history` was provided for this issue. The current HEAD specifically addresses the visible prior concern from the commit message: no `expect("guarded by match arm")` remains in the production TOML descriptor route.

## JIT Issue Management

Pass. Dependency `10ce6962` is marked `done` in `.jit/issues/10ce6962-8973-4357-b836-da8c0b3a6789.json:5`. The issue labels use valid namespace format, `cargo-ci` is passed, and `jit-validate` passed with allowed warnings.

VERDICT: PASS
---
Gate result: PASSED
